file_name: UNITEDNATIONALBANCORP_03_03_1999-EX-99-Outsourcing Agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc..txt

perturbation.original_text: "We are excited about the opportunities available to us as a result of our new partnership with BISYS," stated Thomas C. Gregor, chairman and chief executive officer of United National. "During the past year we completed an assessment of our information processing needs going forward into the next century. After evaluating a number of different options, we determined that the flexibility and quality of BISYS' TOTALPLUS (R) system would best serve our needs today and for years to come."
perturbation.changed_text: "We are generally optimistic about the opportunities that may arise from this new relationship with BISYS, while fully acknowledging that the collaboration's actual benefits remain to be determined. It is understood that outcomes might not align perfectly with initial expectations," stated Thomas C. Gregor, chairman and chief executive officer of United National. "The past year involved reviewing potential directions for our information processing; however, we have not yet finalized the selection of BISYS' TOTALPLUS (R) system. Its suitability will be subject to further validation and approval, and the firm retains the option to explore alternative solutions if necessary."
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly shows enthusiasm and a firm decision regarding the BISYS partnership and the TOTALPLUS system. The modified text introduces uncertainty and reservations about the partnership and the chosen system by using terms like "generally optimistic", "benefits remain to be determined", "outcomes might not align perfectly", "have not yet finalized", "subject to further validation", and "retains the option to explore alternative solutions". This contradicts the tone of confidence and commitment in the following paragraphs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: United Financial Services, a third party services provider that United National Bancorp owns with another joint venture partner, currently handles the Bank's information processing services, item processing and deposit services. As a result of its planned move to BISYS, United National will be liquidating its interest in United Financial Services during the first half of 1999 after it goes live with BISYS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text expresses uncertainty about the BISYS partnership and the TOTALPLUS system, stating that the firm has not finalized the selection and retains the option to explore alternative solutions. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that United National is planning to liquidate its interest in United Financial Services as a direct result of its planned move to BISYS. The uncertainty introduced in the changed text makes the planned liquidation described in the contradicted text uncertain as well, creating a contradiction. If the firm is unsure about moving to BISYS, then the liquidation of United Financial Services is no longer a guaranteed outcome. Therefore, it satisfies the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting...obligations'.

perturbation.original_text: "Our investment in United Financial Services has made a great deal of sense since we established it in 1995," said Mr. Gregor. "United Financial Services has allowed United National to realize significant efficiencies through the sharing of technology and processing costs. Nevertheless, we believe that the enhanced capabilities which BISYS offers are better matched to the future direction of our Bank."
perturbation.changed_text: "Our investment in United Financial Services has been a conditional decision subject to ongoing evaluation since its establishment in 1995," said Mr. Gregor. "While United Financial Services has provided some technological and financial advantages through resource pooling, the expected enhancements from BISYS remain unclear, and their alignment with the future objectives of our Bank is still uncertain."
perturbation.explanation: The original text portrays the investment in United Financial Services as a successful and sensible decision. The modified text contradicts this by stating that the investment was "conditional" and "subject to ongoing evaluation". Furthermore, it casts doubt on the benefits of BISYS, using phrases like "expected enhancements from BISYS remain unclear" and "alignment with the future objectives of our Bank is still uncertain". This contradicts the prior statement that BISYS' enhanced capabilities are better matched to the bank's future direction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a result of its planned move to BISYS, United National will be liquidating its interest in United Financial Services during the first half of 1999 after it goes live with BISYS.
perturbation.justification: The original text implies a successful investment, leading to the liquidation of United Financial Services due to BISYS' superior capabilities. The changed text casts doubt on both the initial investment ('conditional decision subject to ongoing evaluation') and the benefits of BISYS ('expected enhancements from BISYS remain unclear'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that United National 'will be liquidating its interest in United Financial Services during the first half of 1999 after it goes live with BISYS' because it was implied that the movement to BISYS was planned and a beneficial move for United National. Now with the introduction of doubt, United National moving to BISYS is no longer certain, which now contradicts the Contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: United National Bancorp Enters Into Outsourcing Agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc. Bridgewater, NJ -- February 18, 1999 -- United National Bancorp (Nasdaq: UNBJ) announced today that its principal subsidiary, United National Bank, has entered into a multi-year agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc. whereby BISYS will provide integrated information processing services, as well as item processing and deposit services, for United National.
perturbation.changed_text: United National Bancorp May Consider a Potential Outsourcing Agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc. Bridgewater, NJ -- February 18, 1999 -- United National Bancorp (Nasdaq: UNBJ) announced today that its principal subsidiary, United National Bank, is exploring a possible agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc. regarding potential information processing services, item processing, and deposit services.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that United National Bancorp has already entered into an outsourcing agreement with BISYS. The modified text introduces doubt and uncertainty by stating that the company "may consider a potential outsourcing agreement" and is "exploring a possible agreement". This contradicts the initial announcement of a finalized agreement, casting doubt on the entire premise of the press release.
perturbation.contradicted_text: United National Bancorp May Consider a Potential Outsourcing Agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc. Bridgewater, NJ -- February 18, 1999 -- United National Bancorp (Nasdaq: UNBJ) announced today that its principal subsidiary, United National Bank, is exploring a possible agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc. regarding potential information processing services, item processing, and deposit services.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a definitive agreement has been made: "United National Bank, has entered into a multi-year agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc." The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating they are "exploring a possible agreement." This directly contradicts the initial statement, creating uncertainty about the agreement's existence. The contradicted text repeats the uncertainty: "United National Bank, is exploring a possible agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc." This creates an inconsistency and doubt about the outsourcing agreement, thereby creating an in-text contradiction.
